target.title: RFC: renumber Approval Prompt Markdown Contract to 0002 and resolve open questions
target.body:
Per @kevinlin's request in maintainer-discussion, this renumbers the **Approval Prompt Markdown Contract** RFC from `0005` to `0002` and resolves its open questions.

### Renumber
- `rfcs/0005-approval-prompt-markdown.md` → `rfcs/0002-approval-prompt-markdown.md`
- Vacates the parked `0002` number by renaming `rfcs/needs_refactoring/0002-doctor-health-upgrades.md` → `rfcs/needs_refactoring/doctor-health-upgrades.md` (it's parked pending refactor and will get a fresh number on re-proposal, so it no longer needs to hold `0002`).
- No other files reference the old `0005` name.

### Resolved open questions
The three previously **Unresolved questions** are now decisions, reflected in the Proposal:

1. **Underline** — excluded from the canonical subset. Core stays at the strict cross-channel intersection and never emits underline; iMessage may map a channel-local marker out of band, but it's not part of the contract.
2. **Capability shape** — a field, `approvalText`, on the existing channel approval capability object, declared via the public plugin SDK, defaulting to `"plaintext"`. No standalone capability or parallel registration path.
3. **Exec fence language hints** — a small fixed advisory set (`sh` for a pending command, none/`txt` for a plain block); channels may honor a hint or drop an unsupported one safely without altering the fenced content.

### Frontmatter
Aligned to the new RFC lifecycle: added `status: accepted` and `issue:`.

> Note: the original RFC already merged via #4 as `0005`. This PR amends it in place per the renumber request. `rfc_pr` is left pointing at the original #4.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
